action.reasoning: Observation: predict.py returned 0.0 for IC in the previous iteration. Analysis: The issue was twofold. First, the Alpha158 handler in predict.py was initialized without processors, causing it to fail to fetch labels properly. Second, applying CSRankNorm to labels during evaluation alters the raw returns, which distorts the Pearson IC calculation. Furthermore, Alpha158's default label might not be the exact next-day return requested. Decision: I will explicitly set the label to 'Ref($close, -1) / $close - 1' in train.py. In predict.py, I will use qlib.data.D.features to fetch the raw, unnormalized labels for accurate evaluation. I will also use a robust pandas groupby to align predictions and labels. I will increase boosting rounds to 150 and submit for evaluation.
